title: macrolobium nigericum
intro: Macrolobium nigericum, also known as African walnut, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating infections, due to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal preparations, Macrolobium nigericum is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is also applied topically as a paste or poultice to treat skin conditions.
